channel.title: Linear A
channel.itunes:subtitle: History wrote two scripts. Only one was ever deciphered.
channel.description: History wrote two scripts. Only one was ever deciphered. Fifteen-minute alt-history short fiction - a single household, a single document, a single journey, set in a world where one historical contingency went the other way. New episodes appear when they appear.
channel.itunes:author: Linear A
channel.itunes:summary: History wrote two scripts. Only one was ever deciphered. Fifteen-minute alt-history short fiction - a single household, a single document, a single journey, set in a world where one historical contingency went the other way. New episodes appear when they appear.

item.title: The Runner and the Bridge
item.description: 1524. The smallpox that in our world raced down the Andes ahead of Pizarro — killing the Inca Huayna Capac and his heir and triggering the ruinous civil war between Atahualpa and Huascar that left the empire split and bleeding when the Spanish arrived — does NOT reach the Andes first. (A coastal ...
item.itunes:summary: 1524. The smallpox that in our world raced down the Andes ahead of Pizarro — killing the Inca Huayna Capac and his heir and triggering the ruinous civil war between Atahualpa and Huascar that left the empire split and bleeding when the Spanish arrived — does NOT reach the Andes first. (A coastal ...
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Gilder's Afternoon
item.description: 1204. The Fourth Crusade is NOT diverted onto Constantinople — the Venetians and the crusaders sail on to Egypt as the campaign was first sworn to do, and the Queen of Cities is never sacked, never broken, never carved into a Latin empire. Byzantium, spared the wound that in our world hollowed it...
item.itunes:summary: 1204. The Fourth Crusade is NOT diverted onto Constantinople — the Venetians and the crusaders sail on to Egypt as the campaign was first sworn to do, and the Queen of Cities is never sacked, never broken, never carved into a Latin empire. Byzantium, spared the wound that in our world hollowed it...
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Bell of Sotome
item.description: 1614. Instead of the total proscription that in our world drove Japanese Christianity underground and then, after Shimabara (1637-38), tried to exterminate it, the Tokugawa settle on *regulation*: Christian communities in a few Kyushu domains are permitted to exist as a registered, taxed, watched...
item.itunes:summary: 1614. Instead of the total proscription that in our world drove Japanese Christianity underground and then, after Shimabara (1637-38), tried to exterminate it, the Tokugawa settle on *regulation*: Christian communities in a few Kyushu domains are permitted to exist as a registered, taxed, watched...
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Walrus Road
item.description: c. 1370. The Norse of Greenland, instead of clinging to a failing European cattle-and-grain way of life as the climate cooled, begin — through one or two intermarriages and a hard winter that forces the issue — to ADOPT the technology of the Thule (the ancestors of the Inuit) already living to th...
item.itunes:summary: c. 1370. The Norse of Greenland, instead of clinging to a failing European cattle-and-grain way of life as the climate cooled, begin — through one or two intermarriages and a hard winter that forces the issue — to ADOPT the technology of the Thule (the ancestors of the Inuit) already living to th...
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Emperor's Album
item.description: 1605. Akbar's syncretic court faith, the Din-i-Ilahi — his attempt to fuse Islam, Hinduism, Christianity and Zoroastrianism into one imperial tolerance — does NOT die with him. A reconciling grandson holds the line where, in our timeline, orthodoxy reasserted and Aurangzeb's reign later smashed t...
item.itunes:summary: 1605. Akbar's syncretic court faith, the Din-i-Ilahi — his attempt to fuse Islam, Hinduism, Christianity and Zoroastrianism into one imperial tolerance — does NOT die with him. A reconciling grandson holds the line where, in our timeline, orthodoxy reasserted and Aurangzeb's reign later smashed t...
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Relief at Famagusta
item.description: Cyprus / Famagusta, 1571 CE (POD 1571 CE). siege-stone close-third, heat and gun-bronze.
item.itunes:summary: Cyprus / Famagusta, 1571 CE (POD 1571 CE). siege-stone close-third, heat and gun-bronze.
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Year of the Rabbit
item.description: Mexica / Tenochtitlan, 1454 CE (POD 1454 CE). dry lake-city dawn close-third.
item.itunes:summary: Mexica / Tenochtitlan, 1454 CE (POD 1454 CE). dry lake-city dawn close-third.
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Seamstress of Archahaie
item.description: May 1803, Archahaie. As the Haitian Revolution turns to its final war of independence, the leadership tears the white from the French tricolore and binds the blue and the red together for the flag of what will be the first Black republic. The rivet is not the flag — that happened — but what comes...
item.itunes:summary: May 1803, Archahaie. As the Haitian Revolution turns to its final war of independence, the leadership tears the white from the French tricolore and binds the blue and the red together for the flag of what will be the first Black republic. The rivet is not the flag — that happened — but what comes...
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Lacquer House
item.description: 1433. The Ming court does NOT burn the treasure fleets and turn inward after Zheng He's last voyage. The maritime faction wins the argument; the great ships keep sailing; the sea-trade ban (haijin) is never enforced. By the 1480s the southern ports — Quanzhou above all — are awash in private sea-...
item.itunes:summary: 1433. The Ming court does NOT burn the treasure fleets and turn inward after Zheng He's last voyage. The maritime faction wins the argument; the great ships keep sailing; the sea-trade ban (haijin) is never enforced. By the 1480s the southern ports — Quanzhou above all — are awash in private sea-...
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Rider at Takedda
item.description: c. 1585. The Songhai Empire, awash in trans-Saharan gold and in regular contact with Saadi Morocco and Ottoman Egypt, does the thing it conspicuously did not do in our timeline: it buys arquebuses and trains a corps to use them. So when the Moroccan gun-army crosses the desert in 1591, the Battle...
item.itunes:summary: c. 1585. The Songhai Empire, awash in trans-Saharan gold and in regular contact with Saadi Morocco and Ottoman Egypt, does the thing it conspicuously did not do in our timeline: it buys arquebuses and trains a corps to use them. So when the Moroccan gun-army crosses the desert in 1591, the Battle...
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Sverdlovsk Log
item.description: October 1970. The Politburo committee reviewing Viktor Glushkov's OGAS — the All-State Automated System, a proposed nationwide computer network to run the planned economy — approves it instead of shelving it. In our timeline the project died that month, defeated not on technical grounds but by th...
item.itunes:summary: October 1970. The Politburo committee reviewing Viktor Glushkov's OGAS — the All-State Automated System, a proposed nationwide computer network to run the planned economy — approves it instead of shelving it. In our timeline the project died that month, defeated not on technical grounds but by th...
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Berdichev Copy
item.description: Soviet Ukraine / the Thaw, 1963 CE (POD 1961 CE). pavane-elegiac at reading-room scale, Grossman plainness.
item.itunes:summary: Soviet Ukraine / the Thaw, 1963 CE (POD 1961 CE). pavane-elegiac at reading-room scale, Grossman plainness.
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Bayt al-Hikma Auction
item.description: 848 CE. Caliph al-Mutawakkil dies of a fever before he reverses his predecessor al-Wathiq's policy. The Mu'tazilite *mihna* winds down quietly rather than being replaced by the Hanbali traditionalist current. The Caliph's young son is cautious and dependent on the Mu'tazilite-trained chief judges...
item.itunes:summary: 848 CE. Caliph al-Mutawakkil dies of a fever before he reverses his predecessor al-Wathiq's policy. The Mu'tazilite *mihna* winds down quietly rather than being replaced by the Hanbali traditionalist current. The Caliph's young son is cautious and dependent on the Mu'tazilite-trained chief judges...
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Outrigger at Limuw
item.description: Polynesian Pacific / Channel Islands, 1300 CE (POD c. 1100 CE). pavane-elegiac at single-canoe scale.
item.itunes:summary: Polynesian Pacific / Channel Islands, 1300 CE (POD c. 1100 CE). pavane-elegiac at single-canoe scale.
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Midwife of Kaskaskia
item.description: 1175 CE. The Moorehead-phase reorganisation of the Mississippian centre now called Cahokia succeeds where in our timeline it failed. After the great flooding cycle of c. 1150, a new political elite redistributes labour away from intensive ridged-field maize toward a mixed riverine economy — the *...
item.itunes:summary: 1175 CE. The Moorehead-phase reorganisation of the Mississippian centre now called Cahokia succeeds where in our timeline it failed. After the great flooding cycle of c. 1150, a new political elite redistributes labour away from intensive ridged-field maize toward a mixed riverine economy — the *...
item.itunes:author: Linear A
